text: ‘As she has dealt with you’; in the Greek, ‘as she has dealt with others’. If the sentence be understood in this way, it is best to regard it as addressed to the angels in heaven, not to the martyrs.
The word ‘balm’ is omitted, probably by an error of printing, in the Clementine edition of the Vulgate.
‘Between ports’; literally, ‘to a place’, according to the Greek. Some Latin manuscripts have ‘to the lake’, apparently through the misreading of a single letter.
